tier.high: Highest governance obligations. Domain floor rule or autonomy validation may apply. Requires Common + High tier controls.
tier.common: Standard governance tier. Additional controls required beyond the 10 Foundation controls.
tier.foundation: Baseline governance tier. 10 foundation controls apply automatically to all applications.
score.alignment: Alignment score measures how well your governance posture matches regulatory requirements and peer adoption. 0-100. Higher is better.
kpi.pass: Metric value meets the defined compliance threshold.
kpi.fail: Metric value does not meet the threshold. Action required.
kpi.insufficient: No metric data received yet. Connect your app via OTEL to populate.
floor.domain: Your deployment domain has a minimum tier floor — this overrides the calculated score regardless of how low it is.
floor.autonomy: Your app declared human_in_the_loop autonomy but observed override rate is below 0.1%. The tier engine automatically elevates to High.
peer.cohort: Peer group = all registered apps in the same risk tier. Benchmarks show your metrics vs peer average.
alignment.w1: Weight given to peer adoption rate. Higher = alignment score more sensitive to what peers are doing.
alignment.w2: Weight given to regulatory density. Controls referenced by more regulations carry more weight.
alignment.w3: Weight given to trend velocity. Fast-rising controls score higher even if current adoption is still low.
otel.production: Platform only ingests metrics where deployment.environment=production. All other environments are silently dropped.
control.foundation: Foundation controls are automatically assigned to every registered application. They cannot be rejected.
control.adopted: You have confirmed this control is implemented in your application.
control.pending: This control is assigned but not yet confirmed as implemented.
mcp.tools: MCP tools allow AI agents to query the governance catalog programmatically. Requires governance.read OAuth scope.
consent.scope: Controls how your app's data is used in peer benchmarks. tier_aggregate: only aggregated tier-level data shared. full: metric-level data included in peer comparisons. none: excluded from all peer data.
